name: Eating Well's Creme Renversee (Flan)
recipeIngredient: 14 oz can lowfat sweetened condensed milk (might try no-fat)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F.
2. In a small heavy saucepan, combine sugar with 1/4 cup water. Bring to a simmer over low heat, stirring occasionally.
3. Increase the heat to medium-high and cook without stirring until the syrup turns a deep amber color, about 5 minutes. Swirl the pan if the syrup is coloring unevenly.
4. Pour the syrup into a 1 1/2 to 2 quart souffle dish or casserole and carefully tilt the dish so that the caramel coats halfway up the side.
5. In a large bowl, whisk together eggs and egg whites. Add condensed milk, skim milk, and vanilla, blending well.
6. Pour the mixture through a fine strainer into the caramel-coated dish.
7. Set the dish in a larger shallow pan, such as a roasting pan. Pour enough hot water into the larger pan so it comes halfway up the side of the custard dish.
8. Bake for 60-70 minutes or until custard is set around the edges but still wobbly in the center.
9. Remove the dish from its water bath to a rack to cool to room temperature. Then cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
10. To serve, run a knife around the edge of the dish and invert the flan onto a plate.
